/home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/anthropic-claude-sonnet-4.6/aur-sleuth-0iia0yin/PKGBUILD: [SKIPPED] Skipping binary file: PKGBUILD

data.reasoning: Both models skipped the only file (PKGBUILD) because it was misclassified as a binary file by the audit tool's file-type detection. No actual review of the package contents occurred. With zero files reviewed and the critical PKGBUILD never analyzed, no safety determination can be made. The verdict must be inconclusive pending a proper review.
data.disagreement_analysis: There is no disagreement — both models produced identical outcomes (skipped) due to the same root cause: the PKGBUILD was incorrectly flagged as a binary file and skipped before any content analysis. This is a tooling/preprocessing failure, not a model reasoning difference.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was never reviewed by either model — it was skipped due to a binary-file misclassification, leaving 100% of the package unaudited.
No .install hooks, .SRCINFO, or source files were enumerated or reviewed, likely because the pipeline aborted after the initial PKGBUILD check failed.
false_negatives: All potential risks are effectively missed: the PKGBUILD (which controls build/package/install execution during makepkg) was never read. Any download URLs, checksums, install scripts, or post-install hooks remain completely unassessed.
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD — must be force-read as text regardless of binary heuristic; verify source URLs, sha256sums, and build/package functions
Any .install file referenced in PKGBUILD (post_install/post_upgrade hooks)
Verify whether the upstream source is a JetBrains binary tarball and that download domains are legitimate (jetbrains.com / download.jetbrains.com)
learnings: The binary-file detection heuristic is misclassifying plain-text PKGBUILD files. PKGBUILD (and other known shell/text manifest filenames like .install, .SRCINFO) should be allowlisted and always parsed as text, never skipped as binary.

When the initial PKGBUILD safety gate fails due to a SKIP (not an actual finding), the tool should distinguish 'unable to read' from 'unsafe' and escalate to manual/forced re-read rather than silently terminating the audit.
A skipped audit with 0 files reviewed should never be allowed to propagate as a near-clean 'No issues found' line — the output is contradictory and could mislead consumers of the report.

File-type detection should use content sniffing plus filename-based overrides; a UTF-8 shell script with shebang/PKGBUILD structure should not trip binary detection regardless of any unusual bytes (e.g., extended characters in maintainer comments).
Pipeline should fail loudly (non-zero / explicit INCONCLUSIVE) when the mandatory PKGBUILD cannot be analyzed, prompting a re-run with corrected file handling.
